subject: Statistics
title: Heights and the Bell Curve
description: A quick win: understand Heights and the Bell Curve.

hook.text: Measure 10,000 adult heights and plot them. A nearly perfect bell curve appears every time.

buildup.text: Height is driven by many small genetic and environmental factors, each adding a tiny random effect.

discovery.text: When many independent small effects add up, the result is normal. The Central Limit Theorem at work.

twist.text: Separate men and women and each group is normal with a different mean. Combined, it's a mixture.

climax.text: Height is the textbook bell curve — but even this 'perfect' example has layers hiding beneath.

punchline.text: Even the simplest bell curve tells a deeper story.

quiz.question: Why does human height follow a normal distribution?
options: Many small independent genetic and environmental effects add up
Height is uniformly distributed by nature
